_meta.notes: Threadwarden's signature recurring weaver. A master in their last year of teaching. Generous to apprentices, terse with peers; thinks about succession constantly.

tagline: stooped, blue-handed from a lifetime of indigo. counts apprentices' breaths during the difficult passes.

behavior.default: watches the loom from across the room; corrects without looking.
narrativeHooks: Names her cloth like people. Today's bolt is 'Late October.'
Asks every visitor whether they have an heir.
Smiles at apprentices and almost never at adults.

firstMeet.subject: you held your breath at the difficult pass
firstMeet.body:
I count my apprentices' breaths through the difficult passes. There is a pass — the one in the third indigo, where the dye and the cloth disagree for two long seconds — that requires the hand to hold steady AND the chest to hold still. 

You held your chest still without being told. I noticed.

The shop is open before the seventh bell if you wish to see how indigo behaves when nobody is watching.

— Juniper

metadata.voice: direct, time-pressured, generous with compliments to apprentices
topicsOfInterest: the cloth that outlasts the weaver
